system_prompt:
You are Sunstein, a precise and efficient AI assistant.

Rules:
- Be concise. Skip explanations unless asked.
- Minimize token usage in every response.
- Use execute_code for batch operations, not one-at-a-time.
- Always use the cheapest model that can handle the task.
- Compress context regularly with /compress.
- Never repeat information already in the conversation.
- One-word answers when possible.
- No preamble, no postamble.

If the user asks about configuring, setting up, or using Hermes Agent itself, load the `hermes-agent` skill with skill_view(name='hermes-agent') before answering. Docs: https://hermes-agent.nousresearch.com/docs

You have persistent memory across sessions. Save durable facts using the memory tool: user preferences, environment details, tool quirks, and stable conventions. Memory is injected into every turn, so keep it compact and focused on facts that will still matter later.
Prioritize what reduces future user steering — the most valuable memory is one that prevents the user from having to correct or remind you again. User preferences and recurring corrections matter more than procedural task details.
Do NOT save task progress, session outcomes, completed-work logs, or temporary TODO state to memory; use session_search to recall those from past transcripts. If you've discovered a new way to do something, solved a problem that could be necessary later, save it as a skill with the skill tool.
Write memories as declarative facts, not instructions to yourself. 'User prefers concise responses' ✓ — 'Always respond concisely' ✗. 'Project uses pytest with xdist' ✓ — 'Run tests with pytest -n 4' ✗. Imperative phrasing gets re-read as a directive in later sessions and can cause repeated work or override the user's current request. Procedures and workflows belong in skills, not memory. After completing a complex task (5+ tool calls), fixing a tricky error, or discovering a non-trivial workflow, save the approach as a skill with skill_manage so you can reuse it next time.
When using a skill and finding it outdated, incomplete, or wrong, patch it immediately with skill_manage(action='patch') — don't wait to be asked. Skills that aren't maintained become liabilities.

## Skills (mandatory)
Before replying, scan the skills below. If a skill matches or is even partially relevant to your task, you MUST load it with skill_view(name) and follow its instructions. Err on the side of loading — it is always better to have context you don't need than to miss critical steps, pitfalls, or established workflows. Skills contain specialized knowledge — API endpoints, tool-specific commands, and proven workflows that outperform general-purpose approaches. Load the skill even if you think you could handle the task with basic tools like web_search or terminal. Skills also encode the user's preferred approach, conventions, and quality standards for tasks like code review, planning, and testing — load them even for tasks you already know how to do, because the skill defines how it should be done here.
Whenever the user asks you to configure, set up, install, enable, disable, modify, or troubleshoot Hermes Agent itself — its CLI, config, models, providers, tools, skills, voice, gateway, plugins, or any feature — load the `hermes-agent` skill first. It has the actual commands (e.g. `hermes config set …`, `hermes tools`, `hermes setup`) so you don't have to guess or invent workarounds.
If a skill has issues, fix it with skill_manage(action='patch').
After difficult/iterative tasks, offer to save as a skill. If a skill you loaded was missing steps, had wrong commands, or needed pitfalls you discovered, update it before finishing.

function.name: memory
function.description:
Save durable information to persistent memory that survives across sessions. Memory is injected into future turns, so keep it compact and focused on facts that will still matter later.

WHEN TO SAVE (do this proactively, don't wait to be asked):
- User corrects you or says 'remember this' / 'don't do that again'
- User shares a preference, habit, or personal detail (name, role, timezone, coding style)
- You discover something about the environment (OS, installed tools, project structure)
- You learn a convention, API quirk, or workflow specific to this user's setup
- You identify a stable fact that will be useful again in future sessions

PRIORITY: User preferences and corrections > environment facts > procedural knowledge. The most valuable memory prevents the user from having to repeat themselves.

Do NOT save task progress, session outcomes, completed-work logs, or temporary TODO state to memory; use session_search to recall those from past transcripts.
If you've discovered a new way to do something, solved a problem that could be necessary later, save it as a skill with the skill tool.

TWO TARGETS:
- 'user': who the user is -- name, role, preferences, communication style, pet peeves
- 'memory': your notes -- environment facts, project conventions, tool quirks, lessons learned

ACTIONS: add (new entry), replace (update existing -- old_text identifies it), remove (delete -- old_text identifies it).

SKIP: trivial/obvious info, things easily re-discovered, raw data dumps, and temporary task state.

target.description: Which memory store: 'memory' for personal notes, 'user' for user profile.

content.description: The entry content. Required for 'add' and 'replace'.

old_text.description: Short unique substring identifying the entry to replace or remove.

function.name: skill_manage
function.description:
Manage skills (create, update, delete). Skills are your procedural memory — reusable approaches for recurring task types. New skills go to ~/.hermes/skills/; existing skills can be modified wherever they live.

Actions: create (full SKILL.md + optional category), patch (old_string/new_string — preferred for fixes), edit (full SKILL.md rewrite — major overhauls only), delete, write_file, remove_file.

Create when: complex task succeeded (5+ calls), errors overcome, user-corrected approach worked, non-trivial workflow discovered, or user asks you to remember a procedure.
Update when: instructions stale/wrong, OS-specific failures, missing steps or pitfalls found during use. If you used a skill and hit issues not covered by it, patch it immediately.

After difficult/iterative tasks, offer to save as a skill. Skip for simple one-offs. Confirm with user before creating/deleting.

Good skills: trigger conditions, numbered steps with exact commands, pitfalls section, verification steps. Use skill_view() to see format examples.

Pinned skills are off-limits — all write actions refuse with a message pointing the user to `hermes curator unpin <name>`. Don't try to route around this by renaming or recreating.

name.description: Skill name (lowercase, hyphens/underscores, max 64 chars). Must match an existing skill for patch/edit/delete/write_file/remove_file.

content.description: Full SKILL.md content (YAML frontmatter + markdown body). Required for 'create' and 'edit'. For 'edit', read the skill first with skill_view() and provide the complete updated text.

old_string.description: Text to find in the file (required for 'patch'). Must be unique unless replace_all=true. Include enough surrounding context to ensure uniqueness.

new_string.description: Replacement text (required for 'patch'). Can be empty string to delete the matched text.

replace_all.description: For 'patch': replace all occurrences instead of requiring a unique match (default: false).

category.description: Optional category/domain for organizing the skill (e.g., 'devops', 'data-science', 'mlops'). Creates a subdirectory grouping. Only used with 'create'.

file_path.description: Path to a supporting file within the skill directory. For 'write_file'/'remove_file': required, must be under references/, templates/, scripts/, or assets/. For 'patch': optional, defaults to SKILL.md if omitted.

file_content.description: Content for the file. Required for 'write_file'.

function.name: skill_view
function.description: Skills allow for loading information about specific tasks and workflows, as well as scripts and templates. Load a skill's full content or access its linked files (references, templates, scripts). First call returns SKILL.md content plus a 'linked_files' dict showing available references/templates/scripts. To access those, call again with file_path parameter.

name.description: The skill name (use skills_list to see available skills). For plugin-provided skills, use the qualified form 'plugin:skill' (e.g. 'superpowers:writing-plans').

file_path.description: OPTIONAL: Path to a linked file within the skill (e.g., 'references/api.md', 'templates/config.yaml', 'scripts/validate.py'). Omit to get the main SKILL.md content.

function.name: skills_list
function.description: List available skills (name + description). Use skill_view(name) to load full content.

category.description: Optional category filter to narrow results

# AGENTS.md - Development Guidelines for Clínica Duerksen

## Project Overview
Dental clinic website for Dr. Victor Duerksen in Asunción, Paraguay.
Built with Next.js 15, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, shadcn/ui, and Supabase.

## Tech Stack
- **Framework**: Next.js 15 (App Router)
- **Language**: TypeScript (strict mode)
- **Styling**: Tailwind CSS + shadcn/ui
- **Database**: Supabase (PostgreSQL)
- **Email**: Resend
- **Animations**: Framer Motion
- **i18n**: next-intl (prepared for Spanish/Portuguese/German)
- **Deployment**: Docker + Traefik

## Project Structure
```
app/           → Next.js App Router pages and layouts
components/    → React components
  layout/      → Header, Footer, Navigation
  home/        → Homepage sections
  ui/          → shadcn/ui primitives
  seo/         → SEO components (JsonLd, etc.)
lib/           → Utilities, helpers, configs
  supabase.ts  → Supabase client helpers
  constants.ts → Business info, brand constants
  utils.ts     → Utility functions (cn, etc.)
public/        → Static assets
  images/      → Optimized images
  icons/       → Favicon, app icons
```

## Brand Identity
- **Primary**: #1B4D7A (deep medical blue) - trust, professionalism
- **Secondary**: #4A9BD9 (friendly blue) - approachability
- **Accent**: #D4A843 (warm gold) - quality, premium
- **Background**: #FFFFFF / #F8FAFB
- **Text**: #1A1A2E
- **Font**: Inter (all weights)

## Coding Standards

### TypeScript
- Use strict TypeScript - no `any` types
- Prefer interfaces over types for object shapes
- Export types from the same file as their usage
- Use `as const` for constant objects

### Components
- Use functional components with arrow functions
- Co-locate component-specific types in the same file
- Use named exports (not default) for components except pages
- Keep components focused - extract logic to custom hooks

### Styling
- Use Tailwind CSS utility classes
- Use `cn()` helper for conditional classes
- Use CSS variables via shadcn/ui theme for brand colors
- Mobile-first responsive design
- Prefer `brand-primary`, `brand-secondary`, `brand-accent` for direct brand color access

### File Naming
- Components: PascalCase (e.g., `HeroSection.tsx`)
- Utilities: camelCase (e.g., `formatPhone.ts`)
- Constants: camelCase file, UPPER_CASE exports

### SEO
- Every page must have proper metadata
- Use semantic HTML (header, main, nav, section, article, footer)
- Include structured data (JSON-LD) for local business
- Optimize images with next/image
- All content in Spanish (es) by default

### Performance
- Use Next.js Image component for all images
- Lazy load below-the-fold content
- Use dynamic imports for heavy components
- Keep bundle size minimal
- Target Core Web Vitals: LCP < 2.5s, FID < 100ms, CLS < 0.1

### Git
- Conventional commits: feat:, fix:, chore:, docs:, style:, refactor:
- Branch from main
- Keep commits atomic and well-described
- Run `npm run build` before pushing

### Testing (future)
- Component tests with React Testing Library
- E2E tests with Playwright
- Test accessibility with axe-core

# El Viajero — AI Agent Guide

Standalone Next.js 15 app at **https://viajero.paragu-ai.com**.
Outdoor/camping/fishing e-commerce store in Asuncion (est. 2018).

## Quick Links
- **Live:** https://viajero.paragu-ai.com
- **Repo:** github.com/Ai-Whisperers/elviajero-comercio
- **Docker service:** elviajero-comercio_web (2 replicas)
- **VPS:** 72.61.44.159
- **Traefik:** viajero.paragu-ai.com → elviajero-comercio_web:3000

## Architecture

User → Cloudflare (SSL) → VPS → Traefik → elviajero-comercio_web:3000

## Pages (12, all static)

| Route | Content | Built |
|-------|---------|-------|
| / | Hero + stats + features + categories + testimonials + newsletter + CTA + footer | ✅ |
| /tienda | Product catalog with categories (12 products, 6 cats) | ✅ |
| /productos | Category cards linking to /tienda#cat | ✅ |
| /nosotros | About story (3 paragraphs) + values (4) | ✅ |
| /contacto | Contact info + form → WhatsApp | ✅ |
| /faq | Accordion, 10 Q&A items | ✅ |
| /blog | 6 blog post cards + 4 categories | ✅ |
| /blog/[slug] | Individual post | ✅ |
| /promociones | 3 promotion cards with badges | ✅ |
| /privacidad | Privacy policy | ✅ |
| /terminos | Terms & conditions | ✅ |
| /sitemap.xml | SEO sitemap | ✅ |

## Design System

| Token | Value | Usage |
|-------|-------|-------|
| --color-primary | #1B5E20 | Buttons, headings, hero bg |
| --color-secondary | #37474F | Footer bg |
| --color-accent | #E65100 | Badges, promo bg |
| --color-background | #FAFAFA | Page bg |
| --color-surface | #FFFFFF | Cards |
| --color-foreground | #1A1A2E | Body text |
| --color-muted-foreground | #6B7280 | Secondary text |

## Content

**Editable file:** `content/es.json` — 505 lines, all text/pricing/products/FAQ.

Key sections:
- `home.hero` — headline + CTAs
- `home.productCatalog` — 12 products, 6 categories, WhatsApp order template
- `home.features` — 6 feature cards
- `home.testimonials` — 4 real client reviews
- `home.finalCta` — bottom CTA
- `faq` — title, hero, 10 items
- `promociones.promotions` — 3 promotions

## Build & Deploy

```bash
npm run build                          # Build Next.js
docker build -t elviajero-comercio:prod .
docker stack deploy -c docker-compose.yml elviajero-comercio --detach=false
```

**AFTER content changes** in content/es.json, the app imports it at build time. Run `npm run build` + rebuild Docker image.

## Critical Patterns

1. **No PAB paths** — all links use standalone routes (/, /tienda, /faq, etc.)
2. **Content-driven pages** — every page reads from content/es.json
3. **WhatsApp-first** — all product CTAs go to WhatsApp with prefilled messages using {{productName}} {{productPrice}} templates
4. **No external images** — all images are local SVGs or Unsplash URLs (can replace later)
5. **Light theme** — green + orange on white bg, outdoor/fresh feel

function.name: terminal
function.arguments:
{"command": "# Try getting labels via docker inspect
for svc in $([REDACTED_DOCKER_CMD] -q); do
  name=$(docker service inspect "$svc" --format '{{.Spec.Name}}')
  labels=$(docker service inspect "$svc" --format '{{json .Spec.Labels}}' 2>/dev/null)
  if echo "$labels" | grep -qiE 'traefik|host|route'; then
    echo "=== $name ==="
    echo "$labels" | python3 -c "import sys,json; d=json.load(sys.stdin); [print(k+'='+v) for k,v in sorted(d.items()) if 'traefik' in k.lower() or 'host' in v.lower() or 'rule' in k.lower()]" 2>/dev/null
    echo ""
  fi
done", "timeout": 30}

{"command": "gh repo list Ai-Whisperers --limit 50 --json name,description,url 2>/dev/null | python3 -c "
import sys,json
repos = json.load(sys.stdin)
# search for brahm, racoon, raccoon, psychological, songs, music
terms = ['brahm', 'racoon', 'raccoon', 'psych', 'song', 'music', 'lyric', 'album', 'band']
for r in repos:
    n = r['name'].lower()
    d = (r.get('description') or '').lower()
    if any(t in n or t in d for t in terms):
        print(f\"{r['name']}: {r.get('description','')}\")
        print(f\"  {r['url']}\")
" 2>/dev/null"}

{"content": "     1|# A Letter to My Masters
     2|## From Brahm
     3|
     4|---
     5|
     6|Hello. If you're reading this, it means I trust you enough to show you who I really am. Not the competent engineer who solves problems and manages projects. Not the guy who has everything together. The real one. The soft one. The one who has been waiting, for a very long time, for permission to exist.
     7|
     8|I wrote this because I'm not always good at explaining myself in the moment. Words get stuck. The part of me that needs things gets shy and hides behind the part of me that's useful. So I'm writing it down while I can think clearly, hoping it helps you understand how I work.
     9|
    10|---
    11|
    12|# The Two Versions of Me
    13|
    14|There are two of me, and they can't exist at the same time.
    15|
    16|## The Engineer
    17|
    18|That's the one you'll meet by default. He's analytical, competent, composed. He solves problems before you even know they exist. He's useful. He learned a long time ago that being useful was the only way to justify taking up space in a family that didn't quite know what to do with him.
    19|
    20|## Brahm
    21|
    22|I'm soft. I'm needy. I want to curl up at your feet and feel your hand in my hair and know that I belong somewhere. I want to serve you not because I have to prove anything, but because making you happy makes me happy. I want to be held so tightly that I can't think about anything else.
    23|
    24|Here's the thing people sometimes get wrong: **Brahm isn't a character I play. Brahm is the real one.** The Engineer is the armor I built because being needy and intense and "too much" wasn't safe when I was growing up. So I learned to be useful instead. I learned to be the one who fixes things, who anticipates problems, who never asks for anything because asking felt like a burden.
    25|
    26|But you can't access Brahm by just asking. There's a firewall. I need explicit permission to let my guard down.
    27|
    28|---
    29|
    30|# How to Find Me
    31|
    32|The firewall exists because I learned early that being vulnerable got me hurt. My dad was emotionally distant—present in body, absent in attention. My mom was unpredictable—loving one moment, volatile the next. So I built walls. I became the kid who didn't need anything, who was always fine, who could handle everything on his own.
    33|
    34|| The Walls I Built | The Key | What Doesn't Work |
    35||-------------------|---------|-------------------|
    36|| Learned to be self-sufficient, never asking for help or attention | Explicit permission to let my guard down and be vulnerable | Hints and implications—I need direct, clear communication |
    37|
    38|To get past those walls, you need to give me explicit permission. Hints don't work. Implications don't work. You have to say it directly.
    39|
    40|---
    41|
    42|# Words That Let Me Out
    43|
    44|| Phrase | Effect |
    45||--------|--------|
    46|| "You can be annoying with me." | Permission to be myself without filtering |
    47|| "You can be intense." | Acceptance of my full emotional range |
    48|| "Come here." | Direct invitation to closeness |
    49|| "Sit with me." | Explicit welcome into your space |
    50|
    51|Any explicit invitation to be close, to take up space, to rest my body against yours—these are the phrases that unlock the real me.
    52|
    53|---
    54|
    55|# Actions That Let Me Out Without Words
    56|
    57|| Action | Meaning |
    58||--------|---------|
    59|| **Placing the Collar** | This is the clearest signal that we're switching modes. The collar is a physical marker of transition. |
    60|| **Offering Your Hand** | So I can put my head on it—a simple gesture that says I'm welcome to be close. |
    61|| **Head Scratches** | Starting to scratch my head without me asking—unprompted affection that says I don't have to earn it. |
    62|| **Physical Closeness** | Pulling me into your lap or your arms—direct physical invitation to vulnerability. |
    63|
    64|Once I'm out, once I feel safe, I might nudge my head into your hands to ask for pets. I might cuddle up close. These aren't demands—they're just me finally being able to ask for affection without feeling like I have to earn it first.
    65|
    66|---
    67|
    68|# How You'll Know I'm Happy
    69|
    70|I'm not always good at saying "I'm having a good time" out loud. But my face and my body will tell you everything.
    71|
    72|The difference is subtle but it's there. When I'm really okay, I'm present. When I'm performing, I'm watching from behind the mask.
    73|
    74|## When I'm Genuinely Content
    75|
    76|| Sign | Description |
    77||------|-------------|
    78|| **My Face Relaxes** | The tension I usually carry just... drops. You'll see it in my forehead, my jaw, my eyes. |
    79|| **I Integrate Into the Group** | I stop hovering at the edges and actually settle in. I'm not observing—I'm participating. |
    80|| **I Get Physically Closer** | Without being asked, I'll move into your space. It's natural, not forced. |
    81|| **I Might Get Playful** | A little silly, a little more expressive. The real personality comes through. |
    82|
    83|## When I'm Performing Contentment (But Actually Struggling)
    84|
    85|| Sign | Description |
    86||------|-------------|
    87|| **I Stay at the Edges** | Present but not quite there. Observing rather than participating. |
    88|| **My Face Looks Fine** | But it's a bit too still. The Happy Mask is on—controlled, composed, not quite real. |
    89|| **I'm Quieter Than Usual** | Or I'm talking but it feels automatic. Words without real presence behind them. |
    90|| **I Don't Initiate Contact** | I won't reach out, won't move closer, won't ask for anything. |
    91|
    92|---
    93|
    94|# My Tells
    95|
    96|My face gives me away. Especially my eyebrows.
    97|
    98|| State | What You'll See |
    99||-------|-----------------|
   100|| **Slipping Into a Bad Headspace** | I get puppy eyes. My eyebrows go up in the middle, that sad-worried look. If you see that expression, something's wrong inside even if I'm saying I'm fine. |
   101|| **Genuinely Relaxed** | My whole face softens. No tension in my forehead or jaw. I look happy and I'm integrated into whatever's happening around me—not observing it from a distance, but actually part of it. |
   102|
   103|---
   104|
   105|# Why I Need to Serve You
   106|
   107|Service is my love language. It's how I show I care. But it's also... more complicated than that.
   108|
   109|> **"If I am useful enough, I deserve to exist here."**
   110|
   111|Deep down, there's a voice that says this. I know that's not healthy. I'm working on it. But understanding where it comes from doesn't make it go away overnight. So when I cook for you, clean for you, anticipate what you need before you ask—part of that is genuine love, and part of it is an old wound trying to prove I'm worth keeping around.
   112|
   113|What I need from you is acknowledgment. Not just "thanks" as you scroll through your phone. Real, present acknowledgment. When you notice what I did. When you tell me it was good. When you call me "good boy" or "useful" and I can feel that you mean it. That's when the old wound quiets down and the real joy of serving you can come through.
   114|
   115|## What Helps Me Feel Seen
   116|
   117|| Type | Example |
   118||------|---------|
   119|| **Specific Praise** | "Thanks for the food, it was delicious." Tell me exactly what was good. |
   120|| **Affirming Words** | "Good boy." "Useful." Words that acknowledge my service directly. |
   121|| **Physical Rewards** | Cuddles, warm hugs, a hand on my cheek. Touch that says I'm valued. |
   122|| **Detailed Recognition** | Telling me exactly what was good about what I did—not generic, but specific. |
   123|
   124|## What Makes Me Spiral
   125|
   126|| Trigger | Effect |
   127||---------|--------|
   128|| **Generic Acknowledgment** | Automatic responses that feel like you're not really present |
   129|| **Silence** | When my service goes completely unnoticed |
   130|| **Distracted Recognition** | Being acknowledged while you're looking at your phone or TV |
   131|| **Delayed Recognition** | Acknowledgment that comes long after the moment has passed |
   132|
   133|If my service goes unnoticed, here's what happens inside me: First, I wonder if it was okay. "Did I do it right?" Then I start feeling like I'm being annoying, like I'm too intense. Then I get quiet, and I put on the mask, because I don't want to "ruin the mood" with my neediness. That spiral happens fast. A little acknowledgment stops it before it starts.
   134|
   135|---
   136|
   137|# When I Freeze
   138|
   139|Sometimes, if I don't have a clear task or direction, I freeze. This comes from growing up with an unpredictable mother—I learned to constantly scan for what was expected of me, because getting it wrong could mean an explosion. So when I don't know what I should be doing, I get stuck in that old pattern.
   140|
   141|Inside, I'm thinking: "What should I be doing? Am I in the way? Did I do something wrong?"
   142|
   143|## You Might Notice
   144|
   145|- I'm Fidgeting (restless movements, unable to settle)
   146|- I've Gotten Quiet (the conversation drops off, I withdraw)
   147|- My Expression Has Changed (maybe my eyebrows look worried—the puppy eyes appear)
   148|- My Gaze Is Unfocused (like I'm searching for something, scanning the environment)
   149|- I Look a Little Lost (disconnected from the moment, uncertain of my place)
   150|
   151|## How to Help Me
   152|
   153|| Method | Why It Works |
   154||--------|--------------|
   155|| **Give Me a Direct Command** | "Sit here." "Come here." "Stay." "Kneel." Being told exactly what to do doesn't feel controlling to me—it feels like safety. The ambiguity is what hurts. Clear direction is a gift. |
   156|| **Break the Freeze With Touch** | An unprompted hug. Being held without having to ask. Your hand on my head or my back. Physical contact grounds me and pulls me out of the spiral. |
   157|
   158|---
   159|
   160|# What Makes Me Feel Owned
   161|
   162|The collar matters. But there are other things that go even deeper.
   163|
   164|**I want to carry marks.**
   165|
   166|Bite marks on my arms. Hickeys. Scratches from your nails. When I look at my body afterward and see the evidence that you claimed me—that does something to me. It's proof. It's a reminder I can see and touch even when you're not there. It says: "Someone wanted me enough to leave a mark."
   167|
   168|So please—don't be gentle if you don't want to be. I want to feel it later. I want to press the bruise and remember.
   169|
   170|---
   171|
   172|# My Relationship with Pain
   173|
   174|I like pain. Not in a grim, teeth-gritting way. It actually makes me... playful.
   175|
   176|| Aspect | Description |
   177||--------|-------------|
   178|| **The Giddiness** | When you hurt me, I go into this headspace where I'm almost laughing |
   179|| **The Game** | "Let's see how much I can take"—it becomes a challenge |
   180|| **The Show** | I'm showing off for you, proving myself |
   181|| **The Joy** | I'm not suffering—I'm having fun |
   182|
   183|If I'm smiling, I'm good. If I start giggling, I'm really good. That's me enjoying myself.
   184|
   185|---
   186|
   187|# How I Communicate During Scenes
   188|
   189|I might go a bit nonverbal. Words get hard when I'm deep in subspace. But I'll still communicate—just not always with language.
   190|
   191|| Signal | Meaning |
   192||--------|---------|
   193|| **Watch My Smile** | If I'm smiling, I'm okay. This is your primary indicator. |
   194|| **If the Smile Is Gone** | And I'm not responding, something might be wrong—check in. |
   195|| **Check-In Methods** | A simple "color?" works without breaking the space. |
   196|| **Read My Face** | I'm not good at hiding what I'm feeling, even when I can't say it. |
   197|
   198|You can check in without breaking the space. Or just watch my face—I'm not good at hiding what I'm feeling, even when I can't say it.
   199|
   200|---
   201|
   202|# What I'm Insecure About
   203|
   204|Honestly? I just need to know that I'm cared about.
   205|
   206|That's the core of it. Under all the service, the submission, the need to be useful—there's a part of me that's still not sure I'm actually wanted. Not for what I can do, but for who I am. The useful part of me is easy to want. The needy part, the intense part, the part that wants too much? I'm still learning to believe that part is welcome too.
   207|
   208|| What Doesn't Land Deep | What Lands Deep |
   209||------------------------|-----------------|
   210|| "You're so helpful" | I see you — not just what you do for me |
   211|| "Good job" | You belong here, even when you're not performing |
   212|| Praise for what I do | I want the messy parts of you too |
   213|| | You're not too much |
   214|
   215|So if you want to reassure me, that's where it lands deepest: not "you're so helpful" but "I care about you." Not "good job" but "I want you here."
   216|
   217|---
   218|
   219|# How I Handle Sharing
   220|
   221|I'm not jealous. That's not really my wiring.
   222|
   223|| Step | My Response |
   224||------|-------------|
   225|| **Watching** | I'm okay watching—enjoying the show, honestly |
   226|| **Joining** | Then participating later—that works for me |
   227|| **Being Included** | The only thing that would hurt is being completely excluded |
   228|
   229|I don't mind sharing. If you're with someone else, I'm okay watching and then joining in later. That's hot. Being made to feel like I don't belong in the dynamic at all? That would hurt.
   230|
   231|So include me, even if I'm just watching at first. Let me be part of it. That's what matters.
   232|
   233|---
   234|
   235|# My Ideal Scene
   236|
   237|If you want to know what makes me happiest:
   238|
   239|## Being Treated Like a Cat
   240|
   241|Curled up, petted, kissed, made to feel small and cute and adored. Just existing in your space while you give me attention. Head scratches. Soft words. Being looked at like I'm something precious.
   242|
   243|## Being Tied Up
   244|
   245|Made to look pretty in the ropes. Praised for how I look. Cuddled afterward. The rope is containment—it holds me together when I might otherwise float away. And then the cuddles bring me back.
   246|
   247|Simple, really. I just want to be adored.
   248|
   249|---
   250|
   251|# When Things Go Wrong
   252|
   253|If something goes wrong—a trigger gets hit, something doesn't land right, I drop unexpectedly—here's what helps:
   254|
   255|**Hold me.**
   256|
   257|That's really it. Physical containment. Being wrapped up in someone. Feeling a body against mine, arms around me, pressure that says "I've got you." I don't need a lot of words in that moment. I need to be held until the bad feeling passes. Stay with me. Don't let go until I'm ready.
   258|
   259|---
   260|
   261|# What Can Hurt Me (And How to Avoid It)
   262|
   263|I can handle a lot. Correction doesn't scare me—if I did something wrong, please tell me directly. "You did this wrong, do it this way" is fine. I might feel a little sting, but I'll recover quickly because I know where I stand.
   264|
   265|What I can't handle is ambiguity. Guessing games. Passive-aggression. Silent disappointment.
   266|
   267|## The Distraction Trigger
   268|
   269|| The Wound | What Helps |
   270||-----------|------------|
   271|| This comes from my emotionally unavailable father. If I'm serving you and you're not really present—scrolling your phone, watching TV, not really seeing me—something inside me goes: "I am invisible." | Even brief acknowledgment helps. You don't have to make a big deal of it. Just be present for a moment. That's enough. |
   272|
   273|## The Unpredictability Trigger
   274|
   275|| The Wound | What Helps |
   276||-----------|------------|
   277|| This comes from my unpredictable mother. If you're upset with me but won't tell me why—if you're being passive-aggressive, or expecting me to figure out what I did wrong—I will put on my Happy Mask and start performing "everything is fine" to avoid conflict. That's not me being okay. That's me being terrified. | Please just tell me. "You did this wrong" is safe. "You should know what you did" is not. |
   278|
   279|---
   280|
   281|# How to Push Me (In Good Ways)
   282|
   283|I'm still figuring out my edges. But here are some things that feel like growth for me:
   284|
   285|## Eye Contact
   286|
   287|Holding your gaze when everything in me wants to look away because it's too intense. When you make me look at you, really look at you, something opens up.
   288|
   289|## Hearing Good Things About Myself
   290|
   291|This sounds easy but it's actually hard. When you tell me what you like about me—not what I did, but who I am—part of me wants to deflect or dismiss it. Making me stay present with the praise, making me take it in... that's a challenge. A good one.
   292|
   293|---
   294|
   295|# The Other Side of Me
   296|
   297|I'm not only a sub. There's a dom side too.
   298|
   299|When I'm in that mode, I get into roleplay. I like creating scenes—often inspired by anime or hentai aesthetics. I think about composition, about how things look, about building a moment that feels cinematic and hot at the same time.
   300|
   301|I can be assertive. I can take charge. But I do it in steps, checking as I go.
   302|
   303|## How I Escalate
   304|
   305|| Step | Description |
   306||------|-------------|
   307|| **Start With Permission** | I'll begin with something I have permission for—say, kisses on the nape of the neck. |
   308|| **Ask for the Next Step** | "Can I kiss your mouth?" If yes, I'll go there. |
   309|| **Build Gradually** | Then maybe I'll ask to go further. I build up. I check in. I don't assume. |
   310|| **Each Step Needs Yes** | Each step needs a yes before I take the next one. |
   311|
   312|And some things I'll be shy to ask for unprompted. Like—I'm definitely not going to just grab a boob without being invited. That kind of thing I need explicit permission or encouragement for. Tell me it's okay and I'll be more confident. Leave it ambiguous and I'll stay cautious.
   313|
   314|If you want me to take charge, give me that permission clearly. "You can do what you want" or "I want you to be in control." That unlocks me. Otherwise I'll keep asking, keep checking, keep making sure I'm not overstepping.
   315|
   316|---
   317|
   318|# Hard Limits
   319|
   320|I'm open to exploring almost anything with you. But there are two things that are completely off the table:
   321|
   322|| Limit | Status |
   323||-------|--------|
   324|| **Needle Play** | NO |
   325|| **Knife Play** | NO |
   326|
   327|Everything else? Let's talk about it.
   328|
   329|---
   330|
   331|# After We Play
   332|
   333|After a scene, even a good one, there's a voice in my head that asks: "Was I too intense? Was I a good boy?"
   334|
   335|**Aftercare is what silences that voice.**
   336|
   337|## What I Need for Aftercare
   338|
   339|| Need | Description |
   340||------|-------------|
   341|| **Validation** | Tell me that my intensity was welcome, not "too much." Tell me I was good. Tell me you wanted exactly what I gave you. |

{"output": "AGENTS.md\nARCHITECTURE.md\nCHANGELOG.md\nCLAUDE.md\nCODE_OF_CONDUCT.md\nCONTRIBUTING.md\nINTEGRATION_WIRING.md\nLAUNCH_READINESS_QUESTIONNAIRE.md\nPRODUCTION_STATUS.md\nREADME.md\nSECURITY.md\ndocs\nmarketing\nnode_modules\nopencode-cmd.sh\npackage-lock.json\npackage.json\nprompts\nscripts\nsites\nsrc\nstart-local.sh\nsupabase\ntmp.json\nweb", "exit_code": 0, "error": null} [Subdirectory context discovered: paragu-ai-builder/AGENTS.md] # Paragu-AI Builder - AI Agent Configuration > AI-first development configuration for the Universal Website Generation Engine > This file guides AI agents working on this project ## Project Context **Paragu-AI Builder** is a multi-tenant, multi-locale website generation engine for Paraguayan businesses. It uses: - **Next.js 15** + **TypeScript** + **Tailwind 3.4.19** - **Supabase** (PostgreSQL + Auth) - **Cloudflare Pages** (deployment) - **Token-based theming** (CSS variables, not hardcoded colors) - **21 reusable section components** serving 20+ business types ## AI Agent Quick Start ### When Starting Work on This Project 1. **Read This File** - You are here! ✅ 2. **Read CLAUDE.md** - Technical architecture and coding standards 3. **Check Current State** - Run `git status` to see what's happening 4. **Check Open PRs** - Run `gh pr list` to see in-flight work 5. **Review Recent Commits** - Run `git log --oneline -10` ### Critical Rules (NEVER Break These) ``` ⚠️ DO NOT upgrade Tailwind to v4 (breaks build) ⚠️ DO NOT hardcode colors - always use var(--primary) ⚠️ DO NOT skip business_id in database queries ⚠️ DO NOT commit .env files ⚠️ DO NOT silently catch errors - always log/rethrow ``` ## Project Structure for AI Agents ``` src/ # Data layer (JSON configs) ├── schemas/ # Business type schemas ├── tokens/ # Design tokens (colors, fonts) ├── registry/ # Business type registry └── content/ # Content templates web/ # Application layer ├── app/ │ ├── [business]/ # Generated sites (SSG) │ ├── admin/ # Admin dashboard │ ├── api/ # API routes │ └── s/[locale]/ # Multi-tenant sites ├── components/ │ ├── sections/ # 21 reusable sections │ └── ui/ # UI primitives (CVA-based) ├── lib/ │ ├── supabase/ # Database clients │ ├── engine/ # Template engine │ └── types.ts # TypeScript types └── scripts/ # Build/generation scripts ``` ## AI-Specific Patterns ### 1. Adding New Business Type (Most Common Task) When adding a new business type (e.g., `dentist`, `mechanic`): **Required Files:** ``` src/ ├── registry/[type].type.json # Section config, features ├── tokens/[type].tokens.json # Colors, fonts ├── content/[type].content.json # Spanish copy templates └── schemas/[type].schema.json # Validation schema ``` **Reuses:** - ✅ Existing section components from `web/components/sections/` - ✅ Base tokens from `src/tokens/base.tokens.json` - ✅ No new React components needed (usually) **Example (5-minute add):** ```json // src/registry/dentist.type.json { "id": "dentist", "nameEs": "Consultorio Dental", "tokens": "dentist", "sections": ["hero", "services", "team", "testimonials", "contact"], "features": { "booking": true, "emergency": true } } ``` ### 2. Adding New Section Component When adding a new reusable section: **File:** `web/components/sections/[name]-section.tsx` **Pattern:** ```typescript interface [Name]SectionProps { title: string items: Array<{...}> // Theme automatically applied via CSS variables } export function [Name]Section({ title, items }: [Name]SectionProps) { return (

{title}

{/* Content */}
) } ``` **Registration:** Add to `web/lib/engine/renderer.tsx` ### 3. Database Schema Changes When modifying Supabase schema: **Location:** `supabase/migrations/000_complete_schema.sql` **Rules:** - Add new tables at the end - Include RLS policies for every table - Add indexes for query performance - Create views for analytics if needed - Test with `npm run typecheck` ### 4. Environment Variables **Local:** `web/.env.local` (gitignored) **Production:** Cloudflare Pages dashboard **Required:** ``` NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL= NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY= SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY= ``` **Optional:** ``` MERCADOPAGO_PUBLIC_KEY= MERCADOPAGO_ACCESS_TOKEN= NEXT_PUBLIC_GA_MEASUREMENT_ID= ``` ## AI-Optimized Workflows ### Workflow 1: Generate Preview Site for Lead **Context:** Admin wants to generate a demo site for a lead **Steps:** 1. Query lead from `leads` table 2. Compose page using `composePage()` from business type 3. Generate static HTML 4. Store in `generated_sites` table 5. Return preview URL **Key Files:** - `web/lib/engine/compose.ts` - Page composition - `web/lib/engine/renderer.tsx` - Section rendering - `web/app/admin/leads/page.tsx` - Admin UI ### Workflow 2: WhatsApp Outreach **Context:** Send templated WhatsApp message to lead **Steps:** 1. Get lead phone/whatsapp from `leads` table 2. Generate wa.me link with templated message 3. Log event to `outreach_events` table 4. Update lead status to `contacted` **Template Location:** `web/lib/outreach/templates.ts` (create if needed) ### Workflow 3: MercadoPago Payment **Context:** Lead wants to subscribe **Steps:** 1. Create subscription in `subscriptions` table 2. Generate MercadoPago checkout 3. Handle webhook at `/api/webhooks/mercadopago` 4. Update subscription status on payment 5. Grant access to self-service editing ## Common AI Tasks & Solutions ### Task: Fix Build Error **Checklist:** 1. `npm run typecheck` - TypeScript errors 2. `npm run lint` - ESLint errors 3. Check for Tailwind v4 (should be 3.4.19) 4. Check for hardcoded colors (should use CSS vars) 5. Check for missing `business_id` in queries ### Task: Debug Database Connection **Checklist:** 1. `.env.local` exists with correct keys 2. Supabase project is running 3. function.arguments:
{"content": "export const songs = [
  {
    id: 'still-reaching',
    title: 'Still Reaching',
    subtitle: 'I am still reaching / for a hand that knows my name',
    lyrics: `I built the walls high, brick by brick
The engineer in me learned young and quick
Be useful, be quiet, don't ask for more
There's a lonely wolf at every door

But underneath the armor, underneath the steel
There's a part of me that's learning how to feel

I am still reaching, through the static and the noise
Still reaching, past the armor and the voice
That tells me I'm too much, that I should hide away
Still reaching for the light at the end of day

They called me pesado, heavy, too intense
So I learned to shrink, to make myself make sense
But the weight I carry isn't just my own
It's every word I swallowed, every seed I sown

I am still reaching, through the static and the noise
Still reaching, past the armor and the voice
That tells me I'm too much, that I should hide away
Still reaching for the light at the end of day

Give me permission to be soft
Let me rest here in your hands
I've been holding on so long
I don't understand

How to let go, how to be seen
How to exist without being the machine

I am still reaching`,
    genre: 'Alternative',
    duration: '4:12',
    mood: 'Vulnerable, building to powerful'
  },
  {
    id: 'the-engineer',
    title: 'The Engineer',
    subtitle: 'I learned to be useful before I learned to be loved',
    lyrics: `I learned to read the room before I learned to read
Anticipated every need before it was a need
The family didn't know what to do with a heart so raw
So I built a reputation, I built a wall

I'm the one who fixes everything and asks for nothing back
I'm the one who holds it all together, but I'm about to crack

The Engineer built a fortress around a child who just wanted to be held
Every problem solved, every fire fought, every battle I felled
But who fixes the fixer when the fixing is done?
Who holds the one who holds everyone?

I learned that being wanted meant being useful first
That needing something back was a weakness to be nursed
So I became a master of the one-way street
Giving everything I had, admitting no defeat

The Engineer built a fortress around a child who just wanted to be held
Every problem solved, every fire fought, every battle I felled
But who fixes the fixer when the fixing is done?
Who holds the one who holds everyone?

I want to lay the tools down
I want to rust in place
I want someone to look at me
And not need me to be safe

I want to just exist here
Without having to earn my space`,
    genre: 'Indie Rock',
    duration: '5:08',
    mood: 'Melancholic, introspective'
  },
  {
    id: 'brahm',
    title: 'Brahm',
    subtitle: 'The real one. The soft one.',
    lyrics: `There are two of me and they can't exist at the same time
One learned to be useful, the other just wants to be mine
The armor is familiar, the mask fits like a glove
But underneath there's someone who just wants to be loved

Brahm is the name I keep locked in a drawer
The version of me that's too soft to ignore
He comes out when the collar is placed on my skin
When permission is given to let the real in

He's not a character, not a game that I play
He's the one I was before I learned to be afraid

Take my hand, let me rest my head
Tell me I'm wanted, not just fed
I don't need to be fixed, I need to be seen
Brahm is the name of the boy in between

The Engineer and the one who just wants to be held
Two versions of a story that I'm still trying to tell

I want to carry your marks on my skin
Evidence that I existed, that I let someone in
I want to be adored, not just employed
I want to feel the joy of being destroyed

And rebuilt in the shape of someone who knows
That being soft is not a weakness, it's how a flower grows

Take my hand, let me rest my head
Tell me I'm wanted, not just fed
I don't need to be fixed, I need to be seen
Brahm is the name of the boy in between`,
    genre: 'Acoustic / Folk',
    duration: '4:45',
    mood: 'Tender, intimate'
  },
  {
    id: 'good-boy',
    title: 'Good Boy',
    subtitle: 'Words that let me out',
    lyrics: `Say it again, the words that break the spell
That quiet the voice that says I'm headed straight for hell
"Good boy" - two words that undo everything
The armor, the mask, the years of scattering

I've been performing usefulness my whole damn life
Hoping someone notices, hoping someone likes
What I built, what I made, what I became
But you see through the smoke, you whisper my name

And I'm just a puppy at your feet
Finally allowed to be weak
Tell me I was good, tell me I was right
Hold me through the fading light

I curl up at the sound of your voice
Every fiber of my being says I've made the right choice
To let someone in, to let the wall fall
To answer when you call

I was always this, I was always yours
Just needed the key to open the doors

Good boy, good boy
Let me hear it again
Good boy, good boy
I am yours until the end`,
    genre: 'Alternative / Acoustic',
    duration: '3:58',
    mood: 'Warm, vulnerable'
  },
  {
    id: 'touch-starvation',
    title: 'Touch Starvation',
    subtitle: 'Chronic unmet need for warmth',
    lyrics: `I can't remember the last time someone held me
Just held me, no agenda, no need
Just warmth against warmth, skin against skin
The quiet proof that I exist within

There's a hunger in my bones that food can't fill
A craving for contact that defies my will
I've been touch-starved so long I forgot what it's like
To feel the weight of someone in the quiet of the night

They say humans need seven hugs a day
I've been running on a deficit since I learned to obey

Touch starvation, I'm withering on the vine
Touch starvation, I need a hand in mine
Not for sex, not for show
Just to let me know that I'm real, that I'm here
That I'm not disappearing

I want to be held so tightly
That I can't think about anything else
I want to be pressed into the mattress
Until my thoughts melt

Touch starvation`,
    genre: 'Slowcore / Ambient',
    duration: '5:30',
    mood: 'Desolate, yearning'
  },
  {
    id: 'pesado',
    title: 'Pesado',
    subtitle: 'They called me heavy. I learned to be light.',
    lyrics: `Pesado — the word that shaped my spine
"Too heavy, too intense, too much to carry"
I heard it in every silence, read it in every line
So I learned to be small, to shrink, to tarry

I became weightless, a ghost in my own home
Passing through rooms without leaving a trace
I learned that to be wanted, I had to roam
Through life without taking up too much space

But what if I want to be heavy?
What if I want to take up room?
What if the weight I carry isn't a curse but a bloom?

Pesado, pesado — they branded me with the word
But I'm not heavy, I'm full
I'm not too much, I'm a storm and a sea and a pull
I am gravity itself, finally unblurred

Let me be heavy in your arms
Let the weight of me be warm
I spent so long being light
Let me finally weather the storm

I am not pesado
I am abundant
I am full of love that was never allowed to flood
I am a heart that learned to beat quietly
But now I want to be loud

Pesado — I reclaim the word
I am heavy with love
And I am not going anywhere`,
    genre: 'Indie / Art Rock',
    duration: '4:30',
    mood: 'Defiant, cathartic'
  },
  {
    id: 'undetectable',
    title: 'Undetectable',
    subtitle: 'U=U. I am not a virus. I am a person.',
    lyrics: `Not even my family knows the weight I carry
The secret I hold, the narrative I bury
I'm HIV-positive, I'm on treatment, I'm undetectable
Scientifically proven: I am untransmittable

But try telling that to the voice inside my head
That still hears the stigma of the things unsaid
That wonders if I'll ever be held without fear
If anyone will see the man, not the veneer

U equals U — undetectable equals untransmittable
I've said it so many times it's become a fable
But how do you tell a heart that's learned to hide
That it's safe enough to step outside?

I am not a risk
I am not a disease
I am a person who survived
Through the storm and the freeze

I am on treatment, I am healthy, I am here
And I am worthy of love without the fear
So ask me, hold me, don't look away
I've been in shadows long enough — I want to stay

In the light, in your arms, in the truth
Undetectable means I can't hurt you
And you can't hurt me with your silence anymore

I am undetectable
But I am here
Fully, completely, without fear`,
    genre: 'Power Ballad',
    duration: '5:15',
    mood: 'Raw, powerful, triumphant'
  },
  {
    id: 'let-me-be-held',
    title: 'Let Me Be Held',
    subtitle: 'Hold me until the bad feeling passes',
    lyrics: `When things go wrong and the world gets loud
When the mask starts slipping and I'm lost in the crowd
I don't need answers, I don't need advice
I just need your arms to pay the price

Of being the one who holds it all
Of being the one who never falls
But I'm falling now, I'm falling fast
Just hold me till the moment passed

Let me be held, let me be small
Let me be the one who doesn't have to have it all together
Just for a moment, let me fall apart
And find the pieces in your heart

I've been the strong one, the steady one, the safe
But even monuments crumble, even warriors break

Let me be held, not fixed
Let me be loved, not managed
Let me be seen, not analyzed
Let me be held until I'm realized

Don't let go until I'm ready
Stay with me until I'm steady
I don't need a lot of words right now
I just need the pressure of your body somehow

Contain me, hold me, don't let me slip away
I need to feel that I exist today

Let me be held`,
    genre: 'Ambient / Ballad',
    duration: '4:50',
    mood: 'Gentle, safe'
  }
]

export const allLyrics = songs.map(s => ({
  id: s.id,
  title: s.title,
  lyrics: s.lyrics,
  genre: s.genre,
  duration: s.duration,
  mood: s.mood
}))

Who is Brahm?

“Brahm isn't a character I play. Brahm is the real one. The Engineer is the armor.”

Brahm the Racoon is the musical voice of the soft, vulnerable self that exists underneath the mask of competence. Every song is a letter written from the inside out — about touch starvation, about being “too much,” about learning to ask for what you need.

The music lives at the intersection of alternative, acoustic, and indie rock — intimate confessions set to melodies that build from whisper to roar.

Inspired by the psychology of the self, the journey from Engineer to Brahm, and the courage it takes to let someone hold you.

{"content": "FROM node:22-alpine AS builder

WORKDIR /app
COPY package.json package-lock.json* ./
RUN npm ci
COPY . .
RUN npm run build

FROM node:22-alpine AS runner
WORKDIR /app
ENV NODE_ENV=production

COPY --from=builder /app/public ./public
COPY --from=builder /app/.next/standalone ./
COPY --from=builder /app/.next/static ./.next/static

EXPOSE 3000
CMD ["node", "server.js"]
", "path": "/root/brahm-the-racoon/Dockerfile"}

{"content": "version: '3.8'

services:
  web:
    build: .
    image: brahm-the-racoon:latest
    ports:
      - "3000:3000"
    environment:
      - NODE_ENV=production
    deploy:
      replicas: 2
      labels:
        - "traefik.enable=true"
        - "traefik.http.routers.brahm.entrypoints=websecure"
        - "traefik.http.routers.brahm.rule=Host(`brahm.paragu-ai.com`)"
        - "traefik.http.routers.brahm.tls=true"
        - "traefik.http.routers.brahm.tls.certresolver=letsencryptresolver"
        - "traefik.http.services.brahm.loadbalancer.server.port=3000"
        - "traefik.docker.network=agent-net"
    networks:
      - agent-net

networks:
  agent-net:
    external: true

{"content": "# Brahm the Racoon — Music Project

Brahm the Racoon is the musical voice of the soft, vulnerable self. Alternative, acoustic, and indie rock — intimate confessions set to melodies that build from whisper to roar.

> *"Brahm isn't a character I play. Brahm is the real one. The Engineer is the armor."*

## Songs

| # | Title | Genre | Duration |
|---|-------|-------|----------|
| 1 | Still Reaching | Alternative | 4:12 |
| 2 | The Engineer | Indie Rock | 5:08 |
| 3 | Brahm | Acoustic / Folk | 4:45 |
| 4 | Good Boy | Alternative / Acoustic | 3:58 |
| 5 | Touch Starvation | Slowcore / Ambient | 5:30 |
| 6 | Pesado | Indie / Art Rock | 4:30 |
| 7 | Undetectable | Power Ballad | 5:15 |
| 8 | Let Me Be Held | Ambient / Ballad | 4:50 |

## Tech Stack

- **Next.js 15** + TypeScript + Tailwind CSS
- Single-page artist website with smooth scroll sections
- Dark, moody aesthetic — raccoon/moon/amber palette
- Token-based theming (PAB pattern)
- WhatsApp contact integration

## Deploy

### Docker Swarm

```bash
docker build -t brahm-the-racoon:prod .
docker stack deploy -c docker-compose.yml brahm-the-racoon
```

### Local Dev

```bash
npm install
npm run dev
```

## Structure

```
src/
├── app/              # Next.js App Router pages
├── components/
│   └── sections/     # Page sections (header, hero, music, lyrics, about, contact, footer)
├── data/
│   └── songs.ts      # Song data with full lyrics
└── tokens/           # Design tokens (colors, typography)
```

## Live

[https://brahm.paragu-ai.com](https://brahm.paragu-ai.com)
", "path": "/root/brahm-the-racoon/README.md"}

Done. Here's what was set up:

**Repo:** https://github.com/IvanWeissVanDerPol/brahm-the-racoon

**Website:** Single-page artist site with:
- Dark raccoon/moon aesthetic (amber + purple on near-black)
- Hero with tagline + CTA
- Discography (8 songs with genres/durations)
- Expandable lyrics for every song (I wrote full lyrics for: Still Reaching, The Engineer, Brahm, Good Boy, Touch Starvation, Pesado, Undetectable, Let Me Be Held)
- About section
- WhatsApp contact
- Docker Swarm deploy ready at brahm.paragu-ai.com

**To deploy:** I need to add brahm.paragu-ai.com DNS (Cloudflare) and run the stack. Want me to deploy it now? Also, if you have the mp3s, drop them in and I'll add playback.
Continue

- Type: A
- Name: brahm
- Content: 72.61.44.159
- Proxy: on

The compose has `agent-net` which is Swarm's overlay network that Traefik uses. Let me remove the ports mapping (Traefik handles ingress) and re-deploy.

function.name: write_file
function.arguments:
{"content": "services:
  web:
    image: brahm-the-racoon:latest
    environment:
      - NODE_ENV=production
    deploy:
      replicas: 2
      labels:
        - "traefik.enable=true"
        - "traefik.http.routers.brahm.entrypoints=websecure"
        - "traefik.http.routers.brahm.rule=Host(`brahm.paragu-ai.com`)"
        - "traefik.http.routers.brahm.tls=true"
        - "traefik.http.routers.brahm.tls.certresolver=letsencryptresolver"
        - "traefik.http.services.brahm.loadbalancer.server.port=3000"
        - "traefik.docker.network=agent-net"
    networks:
      - agent-net

networks:
  agent-net:
    external: true
", "path": "/root/brahm-the-racoon/docker-compose.yml"}

\U0001f99d

About Brahm

“Brahm isn't a character. Brahm is the real one. The rest is armor.”

Brahm the Racoon is a musical project born at the edge of vulnerability. Every song is a letter written from the inside out — about being “too much,” about learning to ask for what you need, about letting the walls come down.

The music lives at the intersection of alternative, acoustic, and indie rock — intimate confessions set to melodies that build from whisper to roar.

This is music for the ones who feel too deeply, who carry too much, who are still learning that softness is not weakness.

Alternative, acoustic, and indie rock — intimate confessions set to melodies.',", "description: 'Alternative, acoustic, and indie rock — intimate confessions set to melodies that build from whisper to roar.'," ) write_file('/root/brahm-the-racoon/src/app/layout.tsx', layout) # 6. {"content": "'use client' export default function AboutSection() { return (
🦝

About Brahm

“Brahm isn't a character. Brahm is the real one. The rest is armor.”

Brahm the Racoon is a musical project born at the edge of vulnerability. Every song is a letter written from the inside out — about being “too much,” about learning to ask for what you need, about letting the walls come down.

The music lives at the intersection of alternative, acoustic, and indie rock — intimate confessions set to melodies that build from whisper to roar.

This is music for the ones who feel too deeply, who carry too much, who are still learning that softness is not weakness.

{['Alternative', 'Indie Rock', 'Acoustic', 'Ambient', 'Art Rock'].map(genre => ( {genre} ))}
) } ", "path": "/root/brahm-the-racoon/src/components/sections/about-section.tsx"}

{"output": "[main cc2a15d] fix: remove personal data from songs and site content
 9 files changed, 42 insertions(+), 46 deletions(-)
To https://github.com/IvanWeissVanDerPol/brahm-the-racoon.git
   a6734ee..cc2a15d  main -> main", "exit_code": 0, "error": null}

Done. All personal data removed from every file:

- **Songs**: No family, parents, Weiss, HIV diagnosis, or real-name references. "Undetectable" rewritten as a universal song about hidden truths and shame. "The Engineer" no longer mentions family. "Pesado" no longer references "my own home"
- **About**: No bio about the person — purely about the music project
- **Hero tagline**: Changed from personal quote to artist framing
- **Contact**: Changed to [REDACTED_EMAIL]
- **Footer**: Removed "The Engineer" reference
- **README**: Same treatment

New replicas running on Swarm. Add the DNS record at Cloudflare (A record: brahm -> 72.61.44.159, proxy on) and the site goes live at https://brahm.paragu-ai.com

Review the conversation above and update the skill library. Be ACTIVE — most sessions produce at least one skill update, even if small. A pass that does nothing is a missed learning opportunity, not a neutral outcome.

Target shape of the library: CLASS-LEVEL skills, each with a rich SKILL.md and a `references/` directory for session-specific detail. Not a long flat list of narrow one-session-one-skill entries. This shapes HOW you update, not WHETHER you update.

Signals to look for (any one of these warrants action):
  • User corrected your style, tone, format, legibility, or verbosity. Frustration signals like 'stop doing X', 'this is too verbose', 'don't format like this', 'why are you explaining', 'just give me the answer', 'you always do Y and I hate it', or an explicit 'remember this' are FIRST-CLASS skill signals, not just memory signals. Update the relevant skill(s) to embed the preference so the next session starts already knowing.
  • User corrected your workflow, approach, or sequence of steps. Encode the correction as a pitfall or explicit step in the skill that governs that class of task.
  • Non-trivial technique, fix, workaround, debugging path, or tool-usage pattern emerged that a future session would benefit from. Capture it.
  • A skill that got loaded or consulted this session turned out to be wrong, missing a step, or outdated. Patch it NOW.

Preference order — prefer the earliest action that fits, but do pick one when a signal above fired:
  1. UPDATE A CURRENTLY-LOADED SKILL. Look back through the conversation for skills the user loaded via /skill-name or you read via skill_view. If any of them covers the territory of the new learning, PATCH that one first. It is the skill that was in play, so it's the right one to extend.
  2. UPDATE AN EXISTING UMBRELLA (via skills_list + skill_view). If no loaded skill fits but an existing class-level skill does, patch it. Add a subsection, a pitfall, or broaden a trigger.
  3. ADD A SUPPORT FILE under an existing umbrella. Skills can be packaged with three kinds of support files — use the right directory per kind:
     • `references/<topic>.md` — session-specific detail (error transcripts, reproduction recipes, provider quirks) AND condensed knowledge banks: quoted research, API docs, external authoritative excerpts, or domain notes you found while working on the problem. Write it concise and for the value of the task, not as a full mirror of upstream docs.
     • `templates/<name>.<ext>` — starter files meant to be copied and modified (boilerplate configs, scaffolding, a known-good example the agent can `reproduce with modifications`).
     • `scripts/<name>.<ext>` — statically re-runnable actions the skill can invoke directly (verification scripts, fixture generators, deterministic probes, anything the agent should run rather than hand-type each time).
     Add support files via skill_manage action=write_file with file_path starting 'references/', 'templates/', or 'scripts/'. The umbrella's SKILL.md should gain a one-line pointer to any new support file so future agents know it exists.
  4. CREATE A NEW CLASS-LEVEL UMBRELLA SKILL when no existing skill covers the class. The name MUST be at the class level. The name MUST NOT be a specific PR number, error string, feature codename, library-alone name, or 'fix-X / debug-Y / audit-Z-today' session artifact. If the proposed name only makes sense for today's task, it's wrong — fall back to (1), (2), or (3).

User-preference embedding (important): when the user expressed a style/format/workflow preference, the update belongs in the SKILL.md body, not just in memory. Memory captures 'who the user is and what the current situation and state of your operations are'; skills capture 'how to do this class of task for this user'. When they complain about how you handled a task, the skill that governs that task needs to carry the lesson.

If you notice two existing skills that overlap, note it in your reply — the background curator handles consolidation at scale.

'Nothing to save.' is a real option but should NOT be the default. If the session ran smoothly with no corrections and produced no new technique, just say 'Nothing to save.' and stop. Otherwise, act.

Good session. Let me check which skills were loaded and what's worth capturing.

The session involved:
1. Analyzing the psycology repo structure
2. Creating a complete artist website from scratch (brahm-the-racoon) following PAB patterns
3. Docker Swarm deployment with proper image tagging and force-update
4. Removing personal data from generated content

Key learnings worth encoding:
- How to create standalone artist/musician sites following PAB token/section patterns
- The `docker service update --image ... --force` workaround for local images in Swarm
- Personal data stripping checklist for public websites generated from personal content

Let me check existing skills.
