messages.content: {"success": true, "name": "standalone-ecommerce-store", "description": "Build, audit, and deploy production Next.js 15 standalone sites for Paraguayan clients — e-commerce, services, brand/band/portfolio. Docker Swarm + Traefik. Systematic roast fix pipeline.", "tags": ["nextjs", "react", "ecommerce", "tailwind", "docker", "traefik", "paraguay", "audit", "band-site", "client-website", "brand-site", "portfolio"], "related_skills": [], "content": "--- name: standalone-ecommerce-store description: Build, audit, and deploy production Next.js 15 standalone sites for Paraguayan clients — e-commerce, services, brand/band/portfolio. Docker Swarm + Traefik. Systematic roast fix pipeline. tags: - nextjs - react - ecommerce - tailwind - docker - traefik - paraguay - audit - band-site - client-website - brand-site - portfolio triggers: - build an ecommerce store - build a service site - build a website for a client - audit my store - roast my site - fix ecommerce issues - improve my store - deploy ecommerce - new client site - create a business plan - research a business - lead to site - beauty wellness website - generate website from leads - deploy multiple sites - paragu-ai-leads - paralell site deployment --- # Standalone E-commerce Store Build, audit, and deploy production Next.js 15 e-commerce stores. ## Architecture Cloudflare (DNS, SSL) -> VPS -> Traefik -> service_web:3000 ## Tech Stack Next.js 15 (standalone output), React 19, Tailwind CSS 4, Docker + Swarm, Traefik, SQLite (optional) ## Build & Deploy ``` npm run build docker build -t :prod . docker stack deploy -c docker-compose.yml ``` ## Page Structure Home, Tienda, Producto/[slug], Envio, FAQ, Contacto, Privacidad, Terminos, Nosotros, Promociones, Blog (posts + categories + [slug]), Sitemap, Error, Not-found, RSS ## Product Data Structure (Retail) Each product should carry rich spec data, not just name+price: ```json { "slug": "titanium", "name": "Titanium", "line": "Premium", "category": "Resorte", "price": "Gs. 1.800.000", "priceFrom": "2 plazas 140x190", "image": "/images/products/titanium.png", "description": "Premium con Euro Pillow Top.", "sizes": ["2 plazas 140x190: Gs. 1.800.000", "Queen 160x200: Gs. 2.200.000"], "firmness": "Firme", "warranty": "5 años", "weight": "Hasta 120 kg por lado", "colors": ["Gris", "Azul"], "badges": ["Pillow Top", "Antiácaros", "Más vendido"] } ``` Product pages render a specs grid (firmness, warranty, weight, colors), a measure/price table, and badges. ## Retail-Specific Features - **Bundles/Combos**: Section on homepage showing 2-3 product bundles with original price, bundle price, savings, and WhatsApp inquiry link - **Promo banners**: Downloadable from client's current site, displayed as clickable 3-column grid in `#0F1624` section - **Store locator**: GPS coordinates per store, clickable address → Google Maps, clickable phone → tel: link, type badges (Store/Mall/Outlet/Distribución), Argentina subsidiary section - **Product page**: Specs grid (firmness, warranty, weight, colors), size/price table, badges row - **Cross-sell**: "Complete your bundle" section — mattress + base + protector + pillows ## Critical Patterns - WhatsApp-first commerce (all CTAs go to WhatsApp) - Content-driven from content/es.json - Age-gated content (localStorage with 7-day timestamp expiry) - Discreet shipping guarantee (for adult stores) ## Content Structure content/es.json sections: siteConfig, navigation, home, products (14+ with id/slug/name/price/level/sound/safety fields), categories, faq, blog, footer, shipping, payments, socialProof, brand --- ## Comprehensive Site Audit Checklist Run on EVERY page. Be brutal. ### Audit Dimensions: 1. Visual/Design -- spacing, alignment, color, images, fonts, hierarchy, consistency 2. UX/Usability -- navigation, button placement, mobile, loading states, forms, click targets 3. Content -- copy quality, tone, grammar, completeness, placeholders, length 4. E-commerce -- conversion flow, trust signals, pricing, urgency, social proof, stock 5. Technical -- load speed, console errors, broken links, SEO, titles, structured data, a11y 6. Mobile -- viewport, tap targets, readability, nav overlap 7. Industry-specific -- privacy signals, discretion tone, age gate, comfort for first-timers 8. Cross-cutting -- image placeholders, inconsistent icons, duplication, text bugs, keyboard nav ### Severity Triage: - CRITICAL -- Broken functionality, legal exposure, blank pages. Fix immediately. - HIGH -- Trust-killing issues (no contact info, no product images, duplicate nav). Fix this batch. - MEDIUM -- UX friction, redundant elements. Fix alongside HIGH. - LOW -- Polish, accessibility extras. Fix when time permits. ### Batch Fix Strategy: 1. Fix all CRITICAL first (1-3 line fixes) 2. Fix all HIGH second (users notice these) 3. Fix MEDIUM alongside HIGH 4. Fix LOW last 5. Commit + deploy as single atomic batch ### Overview of this session (Fun4Me full upgrade) This session built on a basic skeleton store (Fun4Me, adult toys, Paraguay) and transformed it into a full-featured store matching the production El Viajero store. 25+ batches across: content overhaul, 15 pages, full search/filter/sort/pagination, adult-store privacy features (quick exit, privacy mode blur, anonymous reviews), WhatsApp commerce features, carousels, dark/light mode, PWA, structured data, SVG icons, loading states, product comparison, shipping calculator, product quiz, social proof, per-product FAQ, safety badges, sound level indicators, and a complete 520-item feature inventory. Then ran a brutal full-site roast across all pages and fixed all 21 identified issues in a single follow-up pass. ## Brand/Creative Client Sites (Band, Artist, Portfolio) When the client is a band, artist, or creative brand (no products, no services catalog), the e-commerce template is wrong. Build custom pages. See `references/band-site-pattern.md` for the full pattern — content schema, page component structure, conversion from monorepo, Spotify embed, and common pitfalls. ### Page Structure for Band Sites - **Home**: Hero headline/subheadline, bio section, feature cards (discography, shows, members), Spotify embed iframe, discography cards with album art, team grid (members + role + bio), testimonials, newsletter signup, booking CTA - **Música**: Full discography with album art per item (from Spotify CDN), track listing per album, Spotify embed - **Videos**: YouTube embed cards — extract video ID from URL with regex `/watch\?v=([^&]+)/` - **Letras**: Song title + album metadata, lyrics displayed as lines in a styled dark box - **Shows**: Event cards with date, venue, description — Calendar icon per card - **Galería**: Photo grid with category filter buttons (Todas / En Vivo / Banda / Festivales), hover overlay on images - **Contacto**: Contact cards for each channel (WhatsApp, Email, Instagram, Facebook, YouTube, Spotify) — each with icon + label + hover color matching the platform brand ### Content Structure (es.json) ```json { "siteName": "Nüdo", "tagline": "Hardcore Metal desde Capiatá, Paraguay", "contact": { "email", "instagram", "facebook", "spotify", "youtube" }, "navigation": { "items": [{ "label", "href" }], "ctaText": "Escuchanos en Spotify", "ctaHref": "..." }, "home": { "hero": { "headline", "subheadline", "ctaPrimaryText/Href", "ctaSecondaryText/Href", "trustBadges" }, "bio", "features" }, "team": [{ "name", "role", "bio" }], "discography": [{ "title", "year", "type", "label", "imageUrl" (spotify CDN), "spotifyUrl", "tracks": [] }], "lyrics": [{ "title", "album", "lyrics": [line, line, ...] }], "shows": [{ "title", "date", "venue", "description" }], "gallery": [{ "src", "alt", "category" }], "videos": [{ "title", "url", "thumbnail" }], "testimonials": [{ "quote", "author" }], "newsletter": { "title", "subtitle", "placeholder", "submitLabel" }, "footer": { "copyright" }, "seo": { "defaultTitle", "defaultDescription", "ogImage" } } ``` ### Visual Identity for Band Sites - **Dark theme**: `#0d0d0d` background, `#1a1a1a` sections, `#222` cards, `#333` borders - **Primary accent**: `#8B0000` (dark red) for CTAs and highlights, hover `#B22222` - **Text**: `#f5f5f5` foreground, `#a0a0a0` muted, `#666` metadata - **Typography**: uppercase tracking-wider on metadata, all-caps navigation, impactful headings - **Section gradients**: `linear-gradient(135deg, #0d0d0d 0%, #1a0000 50%, #0d0d0d 100%)` for hero sections - **Spotify embed**: 352px height iframe, centered section with title + caption ### Docker: No public/ dir Edge Case Band sites often have no `public/` directory. Remove the `COPY --from=builder /app/public ./public` line from Dockerfile to avoid build failure ("/app/public: not found"). ### Deployment Same as standalone store: build → deploy via Docker Swarm + Traefik. DNS A record to VPS IP, Cloudflare proxied. --- ## Lead-to-Site Pipeline (Beauty/Wellness Template) When generating sites from lead data (e.g., `paragu-ai-leads` repo), use the generator script + patterns in `references/lead-to-site-pipeline.md`. Key differences from e-commerce stores: - **Pages**: Home, Servicios, Galería, Equipo, Contacto (no Tienda/Producto pages) - **Content**: Services grid with name/price/desc/duration per category - **Nav labels**: vary by business type (peluquería → "Servicios", gimnasio → "Planes") - **Palettes**: Elegant Rose (peluquería), Deep Navy+Gold (spa), Dark+Red (gimnasio) - **Dockerfile MANDATORY**: `output: "standalone"` in next.config.ts or multi-stage build fails ## Auth System: Client → Real API Wiring When upgrading from localStorage-only auth to real API-backed auth: ### SQLite Schema (must include sessions table) The schema in `lib/db.ts` needs a `sessions` table for token-based auth:``` ## Lead-to-Site Batch Deploy Script See `scripts/deploy-lead-site.sh` — reusable script for deploying a single lead site to VPS. Run it individually per site (not in a loop) to avoid SSH timeout on long builds: ```sql CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS sessions ( token TEXT PRIMARY KEY, user_id TEXT NOT NULL, expires_at TEXT NOT NULL, FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES users(id) ); ``` ### Auth context patterns 1. **Always wrap children in AuthContext.Provider** — never conditionally render a fallback during loading/SSR. Children will try to call `useAuth()` and crash. Set all initial state to empty/null values. 2. **Guard localStorage** — check `typeof window !== "undefined"` before any `localStorage.getItem()` call. SSR runs this code. 3. **API helper with bearer token** — define a helper function that reads token from localStorage and attaches `Authorization: Bearer` header. This avoids repeating the pattern in every method. 4. **Auth routes pattern** — single POST /api/auth route with `action` field dispatching to login/register/me/logout branches. Rate limit with in-memory map keyed on `email:ip`. 5. **Addresses/Orders as separate routes** — PUT/DELETE on /api/addresses for CRUD, GET/POST on /api/orders. Authenticate by reading session token from `Authorization` header. 6. **Dynamic export** — all pages that use `useAuth` need `export const dynamic = "force-dynamic"` to prevent Next.js 15 from trying to prerender them during build (will crash with "useAuth must be used within AuthProvider"). ## Docker Build: Typescript devDep Problem The base image `paragu-ai/next-base:node20` has `ENV NODE_ENV=production`. This causes `npm install` in the deps stage to skip devDependencies including `typescript`, which Next.js 15 needs to parse `next.config.ts`. Fix: Set `ENV NODE_ENV=development` in the deps stage: ```dockerfile # MANDATORY: next.config.ts must have output: "standalone" # Without this, .next/standalone/server.js won't exist and container crashes on start FROM paragu-ai/next-base:node20 AS deps WORKDIR /app ENV NODE_ENV=development COPY package.json package-lock.json* ./ RUN npm install --legacy-peer-deps ``` Keep the builder stage at `NODE_ENV=production` (devDeps already installed by deps stage). ### Cloudflare SSL Mode for New Subdomains New proxied (orange cloud) subdomains default to **Full (Strict)** mode in Cloudflare. This rejects self-signed origin certs with error 526. Fix: In Cloudflare Dashboard → SSL/TLS → Overview, change from "Full (Strict)" to **"Full"** (accepts any origin cert). Or generate a Cloudflare Origin CA cert. ACME HTTP-01 challenge does NOT work through Cloudflare proxy — Let's Encrypt hits CF edge IPs, not the VPS. Use DNS challenge or temporary DNS-only mode for ACME. ### Batch Site Generation from Leads See `references/python-site-generator-pattern.md` for the Python script pattern that generates 5+ structurally-identical sites from data dicts. Use when deploying multiple leads from `paragu-ai-leads` repo. ### Lead Site Productionizing (Auto-Generated Site Upgrade) When deploying structurally-identical lead sites from a template (e.g., paragu-ai-leads), they often ship with: - Only 3 pages (/, /contacto, /servicios) - Missing HOSTNAME=0.0.0.0 in Dockerfile → 504 errors - Missing traefik.docker.network label → Traefik can't route - No healthcheck, no non-root user - No security headers, no robots/sitemap - No about, FAQ, privacy, terms, 404, error pages - Scissors icon on all service sections (template leftover even for gyms/spas) - Generic "Transformamos tu estilo" tagline copied across all business types **Upgrade checklist for each lead site:** see `references/lead-site-deployment-checklist.md`. Pattern: same as productionizing any MVP site — add pages + Docker hardening + SEO + content localization. ## Common Pitfalls: ### Deep Business Research Before building any client site, run the full research pipeline — see `references/deep-business-research.md`. This includes: scraping competitor sites for all images, mapping GPS coordinates, identifying related companies/subsidiaries, finding director/owner names via press interviews, and documenting cross-sell opportunities. ### Promo carousels: badge + title + description - Blog category filtering: accented chars need NFD normalization before comparison - Dynamic page titles: reset on unmount to prevent stale titles - Social proof popups: position above WhatsApp float (bottom-32) - Age gates: use timestamp-based expiry, not boolean localStorage flags - Stats: initialize with "---" not "0" to avoid flash before animation - Accessibility: add skip-to-content link with #main-content target - Filter pagination: reset page to 1 when any filter changes - Product images: SVG files in /public/images/ exist but es.json must reference them by correct path. "✦" placeholder means the image path is wrong or missing. - Footer social dedup: if social bar (icon links) AND footer column (text links) both point to same Insta/WA, remove the text link column. - Title template in layout.tsx shows default even with dynamic page titles -- client components set document.title in useEffect, but SSR output still shows the default until hydration. - Price range sliders: two stacked range inputs overlap visually when values are close. Use a single range input (max price only) for simplicity. - Anon review forms: must have `formRef.current?.reset()` + setRating state on submit. Interactive stars need `onClick` handler and conditional `text-warning`/`text-border` classes. - Accented category names: URLs with accents don't match NFD-normalized comparisons. Always strip combining diacritics when generating URL slugs. - Guest mode banner: use localStorage with 24h timestamp expiry, not sessionStorage (which shows on every new tab) or simple boolean (which never shows again). - Page metadata/layout.tsx: the `title` export in metadata is SSR-only. Client-side dynamic titles need a separate `useEffect` with cleanup. - SVG images on product cards: the SafeImage component renders `` tags. Give the container `class="overflow-hidden"` so the SVG doesn't overflow the gradient background. - Filter sidebar scroll position: when toggling filters on/off, the sidebar scrolls to top. Acceptable limitation unless building a custom scroll-position cache. ### Roast Process (Post-Deploy Audit) After any build or upgrade, run this systematic page-by-page audit. The full 18-point checklist from the Fun4Me session: 1. **Promo carousel**: badge + title + description rendered as one word? Badge needs `mr-1.5`, description needs `ml-2`, title needs proper whitespace. 2. **Blog content**: is the full article content rendering, or just the excerpt? If content lives in es.json, check it's long enough. If stored in a code map, verify all slugs have entries. 3. **Blog WhatsApp URL**: avoid literal template strings (`${encodeURIComponent(post.title)}` in href). Must use proper JS interpolation. 4. **Stats animation**: initial state is "0" or "—"? "0" causes flash of broken numbers. Use "—". 5. **Age gate**: boolean flag or timestamp? Timestamp with 7-day expiry is more resilient. 6. **Social proof popup**: does it overlap the WhatsApp float? Position at `bottom-32` or lower. 7. **Footer**: social platform links appear in both a column AND an icon bar? Remove the text-link column. 8. **Blog category URLs**: accented chars (Guías → guias)? Must NFD-normalize before filter comparison. 9. **Contact page**: has hours, address, multiple topic-specific buttons? Not just a single WhatsApp link. 10. **Privacy/Terms pages**: 8+ substantive sections, not 3-4 one-liners. 11. **Product images**: "✦" anywhere on product cards = image path broken or missing. SVGs should render. 12. **Page titles**: browser tab shows product name, not just site default. Check with `document.title` after hydration. 13. **Breadcrumbs**: visible on product/detail pages, not just hidden JSON-LD in ``. 14. **Accessibility**: skip-to-content link present? `#main-content` target exists? Dark mode toggle has correct `aria-label`? 15. **Duplicate badges**: "Principiante" showing in both LevelBadge and SafetyBadges? Only one should show it. 16. **Price slider**: dual range inputs overlap visually? Replace with single max-price slider. 17. **Anon review form**: reset on submit? Stars are clickable? Form resets with `formRef.current?.reset()`. 18. **Guest mode banner**: shows every new tab (sessionStorage)? Use localStorage with 24h timestamp instead. {"success": true, "name": "standalone-ecommerce-store", "file": "references/band-site-pattern.md", "content": "# Band / Creative Client Site Pattern

## When to Use

The e-commerce template is wrong. The client has no products to sell, no service catalog. They need a brand/presence site — band, artist, musician, photographer, creative portfolio.

## Content Source

All text comes from `content/es.json`. No database. No API. Fully static.

The content is typically extracted from a monorepo (e.g., paragu-ai-builder sites/nudo/) and converted into flat JSON.

## Conversion from Monorepo to Standalone

The monorepo stores sites as:

```
sites/<name>/
  site.json         — config (domain, contact, social, features)
  content/es.json   — all page text in builder schema
  content/band-bio.md — raw bios
  pages/*.json     — page configs (for builder renderer)
  images/          — assets (brand, hero, gallery)
  QUESTIONNAIRE.md — client questions
  ASSETS_INVENTORY.md — asset tracking
```

When extracting to standalone:

1. Flatten `site.json` contact/social fields into top-level `es.json` keys
2. Map builder's `content/es.json` keys to the standalone schema (home.hero, home.bio, team, discography, shows, gallery, videos, lyrics)
3. Discard `pages/*.json` (page routing is now filesystem-based in Next.js)
4. Discard builder-specific chrome config (header/footer config — now in components)
5. Images stay in the builder repo — reference Spotify CDN URLs for album art, Unsplash for placeholder gallery

## Common Pitfalls

- **IDN/Punycode domains (ü, ñ, é)**: If the client uses accented chars (Nüdo → nüdo.com), Cloudflare DNS accepts the IDN and browsers send the punycode as Host header. DNS resolves fine, but Traefik rule must use punycode, not literal accented chars.

  Find the punycode: `python3 -c "print('nüdo.paragu-ai.com'.encode('idna').decode('ascii'))"`
  → `xn--ndo-hoa.paragu-ai.com`

  Set Traefik rule as: `Host('nudo.paragu-ai.com') || Host('xn--ndo-hoa.paragu-ai.com')`

  Do NOT use the literal accented char in Traefik labels — it will never match since browsers/punycode middleboxes send the punycode form.

  **Verify**: `curl -s -H "Host: xn--ndo-hoa.paragu-ai.com" http://<vps-ip>/` should return 301 (redirect to HTTPS) not 400 or 404.

- **No `public/` dir**: Band sites often have no public assets → the Dockerfile `COPY ./public` line crashes the build. Remove that line.
- **Static prerender**: All pages are `○ (Static)` in build output. If any page uses `useClient` with dynamic data, add `export const dynamic = "force-dynamic"`.
- **Spotify embed source**: Use `https://open.spotify.com/embed/artist/{id}` with `utm_source=generator`. Width 100%, height 352px.
- **YouTube video ID extraction**: Regex `/(?:youtube\.com\/watch\?v=|youtu\.be\/)([^&]+)/` from the video URL.
- **WhatsApp number**: Store with country code prefix, strip non-digits with `/\D/g` before encoding in wa.me link.

## Verification

1. `npm run build` — all pages compile as static
2. `curl -s http://localhost:3000/` | grep Nüdo — server responds
3. All 7 pages render, Spotify embed loads, YouTube iframe loads
